title: Invocation uses a named struct with custom array serde, not a tuple struct
description: The JMAP wire format for an Invocation is a JSON 3-element array [methodName, arguments, callId] (RFC 8620 §3.2). A named struct with fields (method, args, call_id) was chosen rather than a tuple struct. Custom Serialize/Deserialize impls produce and consume the array shape. A contributor might suggest using a tuple struct (String, serde_json::Value, String) with serde transparent or relying on tuple serde. Transparent on a 3-tuple would produce an array but loses named fields, making call sites harder to read and verify. A tuple struct also cannot carry per-field doc comments. The named struct with custom serde is the right split: good ergonomics at call sites plus correct wire representation.
close_reason: Decision record: current implementation is correct; no code change needed
